title: Golden Garlic Chinese Potatoes Bao
description: A delicious and easy-to-make pescatarian-friendly Fujian-inspired dish featuring potatoes, papillote, and a sweet and savory sauce.
instructions: Wash and peel the potatoes, then cut them into 1-inch cubes.
In a small bowl, mix together ginger, garlic, and scallions.
Place half the potatoes in a steamer basket, then brush with sesame oil and sprinkle with ginger-garlic mixture.
Cover the potatoes with a paper towel, then steam over boiling water for 10 minutes.
While potatoes are steaming, mix soy sauce and sugar in a small bowl.
After potatoes are done steaming, brush with soy sauce mixture and return to oven for an additional 10-15 minutes.
Meanwhile, prepare the bao dough according to package instructions.
Once potatoes are done baking, let them cool for a few minutes.
Split the bao dough in half and cook in a pan or steamer until lightly browned.
Assemble the bao by placing a few potatoes inside each bao, then serving hot.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional scallions and soy sauce if desired.
